name: Zeeland (Netherlands)

colors.symbolism: the field of the historic shield of Zeeland
the lion rising from the waves

description: Three white and three blue horizontal wavy bands alternating across the field, charged in the centre with a yellow shield bearing a red lion rising from further blue and white waves.
history: Adopted by the provincial states on 14 January 1949. The design was created by C. Pijll-Hogerwerff and reproduces the historic arms of the County of Zeeland.

features.symbolism: the alternating wavy white and blue bands depict the waves of the sea (Zeeland literally means "sea-land"), and a red lion rises from them on a yellow shield, illustrating the provincial motto Luctor et Emergo ("I struggle and emerge")
a yellow shield bearing a red lion rising from blue and white wavy bars; the canting arms of Zeeland, embodying the motto Luctor et Emergo
